pages.text:
NorthStar Financial Services
Corrective Action and Remediation Standard Operating
Procedure
Version 1.0
Document Title: NorthStar Financial Services Corrective Action and Remediation Standard
Operating Procedure
Document ID: NSFS-SOP-008
Version: 1.0
Owner: Risk Remediation Office
Effective Date: January 1, 2026
Review Frequency: Annual
Approved By: Director, Risk Remediation Office
1. Purpose
This SOP defines the process for documenting, assigning, tracking, validating, and closing corrective
actions arising from incidents, control gaps, SLA breaches, QA findings, or audit observations.
2. Scope
This procedure applies to all remediation items created in response to:
• Incidents
• Policy breaches
• Control failures
• Audit issues
• Repeat SLA breaches
• QA exceptions
3. Remediation Lifecycle
3.1 Issue Identification
A remediation item must be created when an issue requires corrective action beyond immediate
resolution.
3.2 Action Plan Creation
Each remediation item must include:
• Remediation ID
1

• Linked issue or incident ID
• Root cause summary
• Corrective action description
• Action owner
• Target completion date
• Risk rating
• Validation approach
3.3 Approval
Action plans must be approved according to the NSFS Approval Matrix.
3.4 Tracking
All open remediation items must be tracked weekly until closure. Status options are:
• Not Started
• In Progress
• Pending Validation
• Closed
• Past Due
3.5 Validation
A remediation item may move to Pending Validation only when the action owner confirms comple-
tion and submits supporting evidence.
Validation may include:
• Control re-performance
• Sample review
• Manager confirmation
• Documentation review
• Metric improvement evidence
2

3.6 Closure
A remediation item may be closed only when:
• Action is complete
• Required evidence is attached
• Validation is successful
• Required approval is recorded
4. Due Date Standards
• Low-risk items: target closure within 30 calendar days
• Medium-risk items: target closure within 20 calendar days
• High-risk items: target closure within 10 calendar days
• Critical items: target closure date defined by Director, with immediate oversight
5. Past Due Items
If a remediation item becomes past due:
• The action owner must provide an updated timeline within 1 business day
• The manager must review the delay reason
• High-risk past due items must be escalated to the Control Manager on the same business day
6. Evidence Requirements
Closure evidence may include:
• Updated SOP or policy
• Control report
• Test results
• Case samples
• Approval records
• Screenshots showing implemented change
• Training completion records, if process change required training
7. Recurring Issues
If the same root cause appears in 3 or more remediation items within a rolling 90-day period, the
issue must be reviewed for broader process redesign.
3

8. Roles and Responsibilities
8.1 Action Owner
Execute corrective action and provide evidence.
8.2 Manager
Monitor progress and remove blockers.
8.3 Control Manager
Review higher-risk actions and validate control adequacy.
8.4 Compliance Lead
Support review where policy or control obligations are affected.
9. Reporting
Managers must review weekly:
• Open remediation count
• Aging by risk level
• Past due items
• Validation failure rates
• Repeat root causes
10. Related Documents
• NSFS Incident Escalation Policy
• NSFS Approval Matrix
• NSFS SLA Management Procedure
• NSFS Monthly Controls Review Procedure
4
